title: Watch: Julian Edelman helps Scotland fans place traffic cone atop George Washington statue in Boston
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: World Cup Several statues across Boston have been the subject of the Tartan Army's hijinks over the past week.
Julian Edelman partied with the Tartan Army over the weekend.

Danielle Parhizkaran/The Boston Globe By Conor Ryan June 21, 2026 | 6:35 PM 2 minutes to read Julian Edelman is no stranger to partying down the streets of Boston after winning three Super Bowl titles with the Patriots.

title: Police investigating after person found dead in Roxbury

title: Lowell High School student identified as teen fatally shot in Salem, N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The 15-year-old's death was ruled a homicide, officials say.
The teenager found dead outside a home in Salem, New Hampshire on Friday has been identified as a Massachusetts high school student, authorities announced.
An autopsy conducted by the New Hampshire Office of the Chief Medical Examiner identified the victim as Wichai Saksene, 15, of Massachusetts, the office of Attorney General John M.

title: Will Scotland play a third World Cup match at Boston Stadium? The odds are looking good.
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: World Cup Boston is already saying a mournful goodbye to the Tartan Army.
But the math says that Scotland could very well be playing another game in Foxborough later this month.
Members of the Tartan Army sing and cheer during a school bus ride to Foxborough for Scotland’s World Cup match.

title: Rob Gronkowski and Julian Edelman take part in Tartan Army march at Boston’s World Cup ‘Fan Fest’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: World Cup Gronkowski and Edelman joked that Boston hasn't been drank this dry since Super Bowl 49.
Rob Gronkowski and Julian Edelman landed back in Boston to party with the Scots Friday.

(Lily Hernandez/Fox Sports) As the Scots continue to drink through Boston’s beer supply while celebrating the World Cup this month, a pair of Patriots legends returned to the Commonwealth to take part in the festivities.
